summary: One surgical edit to strengthen the ch17→ch18 thematic bridge. Ch17 ends with Mira's central question ('Do you want this. Not: are you required to keep this... Just: do you want this.'). In ch18's reflective passage (line 61), the reference to the conversation at the table now echoes that question's structure — 'what they wanted, not what was required' — creating a direct linguistic bridge without heavy-handedness. All other seam connections (apartment sounds, Theo's drawing/headphones, the couch, subway route split, the question occupying the apartment) were already well-constructed and required no changes. No next chapter exists (ch18 is final), so no exit seam edits were needed. Opening construction is distinct (specific physical action: coffee maker clicking off) and cannot be compared to adjacent openings since only ch17's tail is available in boundary context. No style bible example lines found in text. All dialogue registers and contraction levels match character profiles.
edits: Line 61: inserted parenthetical '— what they wanted, not what was required —' into the table-conversation reference, echoing ch17's climactic 'Do you want this / not what was required' question to bridge the entry seam
